hypothesis: If diverse scientific foundation models trained on molecules, materials, and proteins converge to a universal latent representation of matter, then embeddings from different architectures and modalities will show high cross-model alignment (as measured by CKNN and IDCor) and comparable intrinsic dimensionality for in-distribution datasets (QM9, sAlex, OMat24), but reduced alignment for out-of-distribution data (OMol25), because the latent structure of matter imposes a common representation across models.
